title: Reflection should not be used to increase accessibility of records' fields
description: Using reflection to increase the accessibility of fields in Java records can break encapsulation and lead to unexpected behavior. Records are designed to be immutable data carriers, and their fields should not be made accessible via reflection.
recommendation: Do not use reflection to change the accessibility of record fields. Access record fields only through their canonical accessors.
impact: Breaking encapsulation of records can lead to security issues, maintenance problems, and unpredictable application behavior.

checks.message: Do not use reflection to change the accessibility of record fields. Access record fields only through their canonical accessors.
